description: Pins the config-create POST body plus Go's port/check/ssl body validation now ported to Python (strictest-wins). Rejection cases set confirm:true so both languages reach the body check despite the confirm-vs-body ordering difference.

cases.name: requires confirm
cases.expect_error: This creates a NodeBalancer config. Set confirm=true to proceed.

cases.name: rejects an out-of-range port
cases.expect_error: port must be an integer from 1 through 65535

cases.name: rejects an invalid check
cases.expect_error: check must be one of: none, connection, http, http_body

cases.name: accepts udp protocol (was wrongly rejected before the proto-enum migration)
accepts ring_hash algorithm (was wrongly rejected before the proto-enum migration)
rejects an invalid protocol
cases.expect_error: protocol must be one of: http, https, tcp, udp

cases.name: rejects an invalid algorithm
cases.expect_error: algorithm must be one of: roundrobin, leastconn, source, ring_hash

cases.name: rejects an invalid stickiness
cases.expect_error: stickiness must be one of: none, table, http_cookie, session, source_ip

cases.name: rejects an invalid proxy_protocol
cases.expect_error: proxy_protocol must be one of: none, v1, v2

cases.name: requires ssl_cert and ssl_key for https
cases.expect_error: ssl_cert and ssl_key are required when protocol is https
